title: France: Loire & Auvergne (Chartres, Chenonceaux, Conques, R. Lot)

main_route.inference_reason: default England-France crossing; no port named on the outbound leg (the diary's transcribed pages open mid-journey already in France near St. Illiers)
main_route.excerpt: That night we camped in a very good site in St. Illiers [?en] Ville Domaine de Inchelin
That night we camped in a very good site in St. Illiers [?en] Ville Domaine de Inchelin (1) 34-76-10-11
We finally made it to Chartres & shopped in the market ... In the cathedral the redoubtable Mr. Miller is still lecturing.
off towards Tours. On the way we've seen a windmill, & stopped to look at it. The sails make a whirring noise.
We camped at Val de Loire ... Mesland. There was a midnight swim to celebrate 14th July.
July 14th. We set off + arrived at Chaumont just in time for church. ... we set off to walk up to the Chateaux.
Chenonceaux. A chateau built across a river. Lovely state rooms & pleasant shaped windows.
We have come on to Chenillé Montrésor, on the edge of a lake - fenced off, so safe for children.

main_route.original_wording: Lake Vassivière, Campsite Pierrefitte, Beaumont du Lac
main_route.excerpt: We finally reached the River Venne ... Lake Vassivière, Campsite Pierrefitte ★★ Beaumont du Lac
we again played at the lake ... walked around Eymoutier for an hour. There are lots of old houses with stone heads on the walls
We picked up food at Tulle, including delicious chocolates — truffles.
We have ended up at this campsite. The pits. We are far from the lake ... [right margin, blue ink:] Lacopelle [illegible] - Vieschamp
We stopped at Aurillac & bought Patisseries & changed money.
In the afternoon we came to Cassaniouge * * * * *. We have a lovely view looking over the lake, with an artificial swimming pool built on at the far side.
We went through the mountains, over the pass, Pas de Peyrol [?Pas], 5000 feet, where Thomas bought an enormous cow's horn
We camped in Viele Compte, a municipal campsite with an indoor municipal swimming pool
We set off northeastwards, towards Roanne, to interview an au pair,
We went on to Laconduière to camp; it has a pretty out[door] swimming pool + good swings for the children.
The campsite was Torcy. E. of Paris. very pretty.
After more trials on the Paris motorway system, we reached Beauvais. The church choir was closed for lunch ... The church is amazingly high
In the afternoon we stopped at Crécy & stood on the site of King Edward's windmill & looked down the hill where English longbowmen had wiped out the flower of French [?Chivalry/Cavalry].
That evening we camped at Beauvainville. We found it pretty, near a stream, flat, crowded, trees.

main_route.inference_reason: default England-France crossing; return ferry mentioned but no port named
main_route.excerpt: We stopped at a supermarket & caught the ferry. The children watched cartoons.
We drove home in the rain. A very good holiday.

crossings.excerpt: That night we camped in a very good site in St. Illiers [?en] Ville Domaine de Inchelin
We stopped at a supermarket & caught the ferry. The children watched cartoons. We drove home in the rain.

excursions.label: Anniversary day - attempted Conques (blocked by motor rally), merry-go-round village, Entraigues

stops.excerpt: We set off to see Conques ... Cars were parked so densely near Conques that we had to give up going there
We decided to go home via Entraigues, only to find our way blocked by the rally.

excursions.label: Sunday - church at Cassaniouze, coffee at Château la Rothe, lunch at Calvinet

stops.normalized_name: Château la Rothe, near Cassaniouze, France
stops.excerpt: We parked outside Chateau la Rothe for coffee - very symmetrical house with beautiful farm buildings, clock tower & chapel & barns.
We went out to supper in Calvinet, at Hotel de Beauségou. One of the most delicious meals

excursions.label: Day trip to Conques (successful) and dinner at Hôtel du Nord, Mur-de-Barrez

stops.excerpt: Today we actually made it to Conques looked round the shops + church + had lunch in a friendly restaurant ... The tympanum is awe-inspiring

excursions.label: Day trip to Estaing and Espalion

stops.excerpt: We set off early on a day trip to see Estaing and Espalion. Lovely old houses, chateaux + churches.
We set off early on a day trip to see Estaing and Espalion. Lovely old houses, chateaux + churches.

excursions.label: Kayak day on the River Lot from Entraygues

stops.excerpt: Simon, Thomas, William + Peter set off at 9.10. in 2 double Kayaks ... taking them all 5 miles up-river to Entraygues to shoot the rapids down to here.

unresolved.excerpt: In the evening we went to Hotel du Nord at Montealey. It was described in the book as "cosy".

unresolved.issue: restaurant/locality near the River Lot used on the anniversary/canoe days; likely Vieillevie (Cantal) on the Lot but spelling uncertain; recorded here rather than guessed into the route

boundary_flags.issue: Trip year ambiguous. trip_id and bundle date_guess say 1991, but per-page ISO date guesses in the source are inconsistent (1989, 1992, 1993). Diary day/month dates (July 13th-30th) are internally consistent; year taken as 1991 per the given trip_id.

mixed_end flagged in bundle: A_0052_R is a boundary page ending this trip (return ferry + drive home). The outbound start is also mid-journey - the transcribed pages open already in France near St. Illiers, so the home departure and outbound crossing are inferred.

summary: A July 1991 family camping holiday from Birmingham across the Channel to France, touring the Loire (Chartres, a Beauce windmill, Chaumont-sur-Loire, Chenonceau) before settling into lake bases at Vassivière and then a five-star site at Cassaniouze in the Auvergne. From Cassaniouze they made day trips to Conques, Estaing, Espalion and a kayak run on the River Lot from Entraygues. They returned north over the Pas de Peyrol via Vic-le-Comte, Roanne, Paris (Torcy), Beauvais and the Crécy battlefield before catching the ferry home on 30 July.
extraction_notes: No genuine route sketch exists: the two sketch pages (A_0042_R, A_0046_R) are an architectural drawing of Chartres Cathedral and a scenic panorama of the Auvergne volcanoes respectively, with no arrows or route lines - confirmed by reading both images. Route therefore reconstructed from dated narrative (evidence level 2). Cassaniouze (seq 15) is a fixed base occupied roughly 19-26 July; the anniversary day, the July 21st church/chateau day, the July 22nd Conques visit, the July 24th Estaing/Espalion trip and the July 25th Lot kayak day all return to it and are recorded as excursions, not flattened into the main route. Both Channel crossings are inferred dover-calais (no port named either direction); confidence 0.5 each. The July 25th heading on A_0051_L is out of sequence (it follows July 27th content) and is treated as a transcription/labelling slip. Several minor localities (Laconduière, Beauvainville, Montealey, Viellievie) could not be confidently geocoded and are listed under unresolved rather than guessed.
